Daily Challenges:

Consistency is key to success, and you’re on the road to it by coming back to REDSEC. Check your inbox for tips on how to win your next Battle Royale or Gauntlet match, but in general:
- Play the objective.
- Revive your squadmates.
- Know your role, or rather, your Class and the strengths of your weapon loadouts
- Never give up; every loss is an opportunity to learn and create that winning strategy.

Welcome to Battlefield REDSEC; thank you for joining our community in this free-to-play experience.

Here are 3 tips to get you started:

1. SET UP YOUR SETTINGS FOR SUCCESS. At the right end of the main menu are your Settings, which include Sensitivity sliders, button mapping or keybinds and more. You can then head to the Firing Range - found either in the main Play menu or when editing a Loadout - to practice movement, aiming, firing, and reloading.

2. TRY BOTH GAUNTLET AND BATTLE ROYALE. REDSEC has two game modes, and both can help you learn how to win matches. Try Battle Royale to learn how to pick your engagements, or give Gauntlet a shot and see how playing the objective can win matches.

3. USE THE ENVIRONMENT TO YOUR ADVANTAGE. Man-made structures - even the massive downtown buildings - are meant to be destroyed. Use explosives, vehicles, and bullets to level advantages an enemy may have in terms of cover or high ground.

Fight on one side of a frontline battle for territory. As attackers, capture objectives to take enemy sectors. As defenders, hold your positions and stop the enemy advance.

ATTACKERS – SECURE ALL SECTORS
- Capture objectives in all sectors before running out of lives or the time expires
- Keep control of both objectives in a sector to capture it. Then, move on to the next sector 
- Retain lives by reviving your teammates
- Earn reinforcements after taking each sector
- Take final sector to win the battle

DEFENDERS – STOP THE ENEMY ADVANCE
- Defend each sector with unlimited lives 
- Stop the enemy from taking all sectors or the battle is lost
- Win by fully depleting the attacker’s lives before they take all sectors

You put yourself through the Gauntlet; that’s half the battle. The other is winning consistently. Here’s three tips on how you can do just that:

1. PLAY THE OBJECTIVE. We’ll say it politely: above all else, complete objective-based tasks and treat eliminating enemies as a secondary goal… Unless it’s a Contract or Vendetta mission, of course.

2. REVIVE TO STAY ALIVE. Reviving squadmates gives your squad one point, gets your squadmate back in the fight, denies the enemy a kill, and prevents a lengthy respawn delay. Whenever possible, make it a point to revive your squadmates.

3. THE POINTS DO MATTER. DOUBLE, IN FACT, IN THE FINAL MINUTE. Whether you hold off on capturing a key objective or set up an attack when the clock hits one minute remaining, try working this magic minute into your strategies and pull off those “unexpected” comebacks.

Nice work in your first Battle Royale match! If you’re ready to win, then consider these three tips for success in your next match:

1. YOUR MISSION: TAKE ON MISSIONS. Missions allow you to earn better weapons, including your own via Custom Weapon Drop, powerful game-changing Strike Packages, and even Vehicle Keycards to gain access to a tank. Accept Missions by following the on-screen command as soon as they become available.

2. KNOW YOUR ROLE. Every Class has its own unique abilities, Gadgets, and Signature Weapon. Whether you are the first to the fight with Assault, handling or dismantling vehicles with the Engineer, resupplying and reviving squadmates as Support, or scouting ahead with Recon, knowing your Class’ strengths is pivotal to success.

3. TAKEDOWNS LEAD TO FLAWLESS EXECUTION. If an enemy is down and no other threats are nearby, perform a takedown to reveal their squad’s nearby position, which help you and your squad take a better position for a potential squad wipe.

In Battlefield, you can level up your Career Rank by earning XP and completing challenges. Career Rank is separate from any Mastery Ranks, and has its own rewards for certain milestones.
